categories.label: Establishing
categories.description: Sets context — wide of a location, character intro, world snapshot. Usually slower, often the first shot of an act.

categories.label: Action — peak intensity
categories.description: Combat impact, weapon discharge, explosion, hard physical hit. Belongs on drops and in high-energy zones. Sub-second cuts work here.

categories.label: Action — sustained motion
categories.description: Chase, footwork, parry-and-step, kinetic but not climactic. Pre/post drop, mid energy zones. 0.6-1.5s typical.

categories.label: Reaction — quiet
categories.description: Character reacts subtly — eye-flick, half-smile, gaze. Low motion. Belongs in instrumental valleys and after drops.

categories.label: Reaction — overt emotion
categories.description: Tears, scream, breaking, laughing, raw recognition. Carries emotional payload directly. Pairs with emotional swells in the song.

categories.label: Dialogue — character speaking
categories.description: Character is delivering an active line on-screen, mouth visible. Goes in DIALOGUE_SAFE windows. Lip-sync plausibility scored separately.

categories.label: Dialogue — listener / reaction shot
categories.description: Character listening to dialogue (theirs or another's). No active mouth movement. Useful for cutaways during long lines.

categories.label: Transitional — cover the cut
categories.description: Whip pan, light leak, motion blur, match-cut hinge. Doesn't carry meaning on its own; bridges two scenes.

categories.label: Climactic — peak narrative moment
categories.description: The defining shot — hero turn, recognition, decisive blow, last embrace. Only ONE per act usually. Hold longer than its duration suggests.

categories.label: Resolution — release / aftermath
categories.description: Comes down from the climax. Walking away, embrace held, fade-out, post-fight breath. Belongs in breakdowns and final acts.

categories.label: Texture — b-roll / pattern
categories.description: Insert shot with no specific narrative weight — flickering lights, hands, environmental detail. Used to create rhythm or buy editorial breath. Cheap to drop or extend.
